message:
So last week I realized our smoke alarms were due for their annual battery swap, and honestly? I turned it into a tiny family event instead of a chore. We gathered in the kitchen with fresh cookies (yes, really), I showed my kids how to test each alarm, and we actually talked through our escape plan while munching. My partner even laughed when I made a terrible joke about "smoke detectors detecting smoke" — which, okay, was bad, but the point is it didn't feel like doom prep. It felt like... caring for our home together?

I think that's the secret, isn't it? These things don't have to be scary or stressful. A quick refresh of your smoke alarms, knowing two ways out of each room, keeping the kitchen clear of clutter — it's all just normal home maintenance wrapped in a little intentionality. This little refresher actually helped me remember that.

Have you done your smoke alarm check recently, or is it on the to-do pile? 🏡
